compiled_state.title: Code reviewer agents use wrong dimension names in reviewer-findings.json (recurring)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Problem
Code reviewer sub-agents (particularly dso:code-reviewer-light with haiku) intermittently output reviewer-findings.json with incorrect dimension names. Expected: correctness, verification, hygiene, design, maintainability. Observed: code_hygiene, object_oriented_design, readability, functionality, testing_coverage.

This causes record-review.sh to reject the findings with 'missing score dimension' errors, requiring manual correction of the JSON and hash recalculation.

## Recurring Pattern
This has been observed multiple times during sprint execution. The root cause likely involves:
1. The reviewer agent prompt not being specific enough about the exact dimension key names
2. The agent substituting semantically similar but incorrect names
3. No validation step between the reviewer agent producing output and record-review.sh consuming it

## Suggested Investigation
- Check agent prompt templates in plugins/dso/agents/ for dimension name specificity
- Consider adding a pre-validation step that normalizes dimension names before hashing
- Consider adding the exact JSON schema to the reviewer prompt as a hard constraint
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
